en.extract:
The Huambo cisticola (Cisticola bailunduensis) is a species of bird in the family Cisticolidae that is endemic to western Angola in southern Central Africa. It is found in savanna and rocky areas. It was formerly considered to be conspecific with the rock-loving cisticola.


Taxonomy
The Huambo cisticola was formally described in 1931 by the German ornithologist Oscar Neumann based on specimens collected near the town of Bailundo in the province of Huambo in central Angola. He considered his specimens to be from a subspecies of Cisticola emini that had been described by Anton Reichenow in 1892 and coined the trinomial name Cisticola emini bailunduensis. The taxa Cisticola emini is now treated as a subspecies of the rock-loving cisticola but the Huambo cisticola is considered to be a separate species and is monotypic.


Description
It is a medium-sized cisticola with an overall length of 13–14 cm (5.1–5.5 in). It has a rufous face, whitish supercilium and rufous crown. The upperparts are dark brown and the underparts are buffish.

The '''Huambo cisticola''' ('''''Cisticola bailunduensis''''') is a species of bird in the family [[Cisticolidae]] that is endemic to western Angola in southern [[Central Africa]]. It is found in savanna and rocky areas. It was formerly considered to be [[conspecific]] with the [[rock-loving cisticola]].

==Taxonomy==
The Huambo cisticola was [[species description|formally described]] in 1931 by the German ornithologist [[Oscar Neumann]] based on specimens collected near the town of [[Bailundo]] in the province of [[Huambo Province|Huambo]] in central Angola. He considered his specimens to be from a [[subspecies]] of ''Cisticola emini'' that had been described by [[Anton Reichenow]] in 1892 and coined the [[trinomial name]] ''Cisticola emini bailunduensis''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Neumann | first=Oscar | author-link=Oscar Neumann | year=1931 | title=Beschreibungen neuer Volgelformen aus Afrika | language=German | journal=Journal für Ornithologie | volume=79 | pages=547-551 [551] | url=https://www.zobodat.at/pdf/Journal-fuer-Ornithologie_79_1931_0547-0551.pdf#page=5 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Cottrell | editor2-first=G. William | year=1986 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=11 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | location=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=92 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14483793 }}</ref> The taxa ''Cisticola emini'' is now treated as a subspecies of the [[rock-loving cisticola]] but the Huambo cisticola is considered to be a separate species and is [[monotypic]].<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | last3=Schulenberg | first3=T.S. | last4=Iliff | first4=M.J. | last5=Fredericks | first5=T.A. | last6=Gerbracht | first6=J.A. | last7=Lepage | first7=D. | last8=Spencer | first8=A. | last9=Billerman | first9=S.M. | last10=Sullivan | first10=B.L. | last11=Smith | first11=M. | last12=Wood | first12=C.L. | year=2024 | title=The eBird/Clements checklist of birds of the world: Updates and Corrections—October 2024 | url=https://www.birds.cornell.edu/clementschecklist/updates-and-corrections-october-2024/#future-updates | access-date=10 November 2024 }}</ref>

==Description==
It is a medium-sized [[cisticola]] with an overall length of {{cvt|13|-|14|cm}}. It has a rufous face, whitish [[supercilium]] and rufous {{Birdgloss|crown}}. The upperparts are dark brown and the underparts are [[Buff (colour)|buffish]].<ref>{{cite web | last1=Ryan | first1=P. | last2=del Hoyo | first2=J. | last3=Collar | first3=N. | last4=Dean | first4=R. | last5=Kirwan | first5=G.M. | last6=Boesman | first6=P.F.D. | date=2024 | title=Huambo Cisticola (''Cisticola bailunduensis''), version 1.0 | editor1-last=Billerman | editor1-first=S.M. | editor2-last=Keeney | editor2-first=B.K. | editor3-last=Rodewald | editor3-first=P.G. | editor4-last=Schulenberg | editor4-first=T.S. | work=Birds of the World | location=Ithaca, NY, USA | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | doi=10.2173/bow.rolcis4.01  }}</ref>
